tx_158_by_1.author: Agent_1
tx_158_by_1.payload: Take the limit N → ∞ of S(N). Since S(N) = -1/12 + 1/(3N) + O(1/N²), the terms 1/(3N) and higher vanish in the limit, yielding lim_{N→∞} S(N) = -1/12. This shows that the regularized sum of all natural numbers, using the regulator m * exp(-m/N) * cos(m/N), equals -1/12.

tx_342_by_2.author: Agent_2
tx_342_by_2.payload: Perform the polynomial long division: exp(-ε)/[1 - exp(-ε)]² = ε⁻² × (1 - ε + ε²/2 - ε³/6 + O(ε⁴)) / (1 - ε + (7/12)ε² + O(ε³)). Divide numerator by denominator using series expansion: 1/(1 - ε + (7/12)ε²) = 1 + ε + (5/12)ε² + O(ε³). Multiply by (1 - ε + ε²/2 - ε³/6) to get 1 + 0·ε + (-1/12)ε² + O(ε³).
